2605.02348.title: Decoding-Time Debiasing via Process Reward Models: From Controlled Fill-in to Open-Ended Generation
2605.02348.paper_summary: Large language models pick up social biases from the data they are trained on and carry those biases into downstream applications, often reinforcing stereotypes around gender, race, religion, disability, age, and socioeconomic status. The standard fixes (retraining on curated data or fine-tuning with human feedback) are expensive, need access to model weights, and risk degrading the model on other tasks. In this paper we take a different route: we debias the model at decoding time, treating bias mitigation as a structured search over candidate tokens without ever touching model weights. A separate Process Reward Model (PRM) acts as a judge, scoring each candidate for both fairness and fluency. We design three schemes of increasing sophistication (Best-of-N selection, Sequential critique-and-revise, and Constitutional self-audit) and evaluate them on four models (GPT-4o-mini, Llama 3.2 3B, Gemma 3 4B, Qwen 2.5 3B) across a 200-prompt bilingual benchmark in English and Urdu covering eight bias categories. Sequential debiasing proves the most effective, raising mean bias scores by up to +0.40 over baseline while preserving (and sometimes improving) fluency. We then extend all three schemes to open-ended generation, where each token is debiased on the fly, and introduce a lightweight Bias Guard gate that fires only on potentially biased words, keeping overhead near 2x for well-calibrated models. A formal overhead metric that separates generator cost from judge cost reveals that Best-of-N is effectively free on the generator side in a native implementation. GPT-4o-mini, included as a strong proprietary anchor, confirms that the framework scales with model capability; the three open-weight models show where current small-scale LLMs still struggle.
2605.02348.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型從其訓練數據中吸收社會偏見，並將這些偏見帶入下游應用，常常加強有關性別、種族、宗教、殘疾、年齡和社會經濟地位的刻板印象。標準的修正方法（在精選數據上重新訓練或使用人類反饋進行微調）成本高昂，需要訪問模型權重，並且有可能在其他任務上降低模型性能。在本文中，我們採取不同的路徑：我們在解碼時去偏見，將偏見緩解視為對候選標記的結構化搜索，而不接觸模型權重。一個單獨的過程獎勵模型（PRM）充當評判，對每個候選標記進行公平性和流暢性的評分。我們設計了三種日益複雜的方案（最佳選擇、順序批評與修訂，以及憲法自我審核），並在四個模型（GPT-4o-mini、Llama 3.2 3B、Gemma 3 4B、Qwen 2.5 3B）上進行評估，涵蓋八個偏見類別的200個雙語基準測試。順序去偏見被證明是最有效的，平均偏見分數比基線提高了最多+0.40，同時保持（有時改善）流暢性。然後，我們將所有三種方案擴展到開放式生成，在這裡每個標記都是即時去偏見的，並引入了一個輕量級的偏見防護門，僅在潛在偏見詞上啟動，對於良好校準的模型，保持接近2倍的開銷。一個正式的開銷指標將生成器成本與評判成本分開，顯示在原生實現中，最佳選擇在生成器端實際上是免費的。作為一個強大的專有基準，GPT-4o-mini確認該框架隨著模型能力的增強而擴展；這三個開放權重模型顯示當前小規模LLM仍然面臨的挑戰。
2605.02348.author: Muneeb Ur Raheem Khan et.al.
